lead: Figures on this page are verified against the sources below.
Note: on the Live engine there is no longer a selectable bio/geo site in the left panel — use coordinate navigation (or EDISON / EDbearing). Raw materials cap at G4 (150).

sources.label: Frontier Forums
sources.what: Community shard circuit & the HIP 36601 / Outotz jackpot coordinates this loop uses.

sources.label: AXI Wiki
sources.what: Engineering-materials gathering: fill-the-top, trade-down method and shard DSS technique.

sources.label: Inara — Crystal Shards
sources.what: Database component entry for Crystal Shards — the salvageable raw material tied to the shard sites.

sources.label: Fandom Wiki
sources.what: Crystalline Shard mechanics — G4 raw drops, 12,000 Ls distance gating, Sanguineous Rim sites.

sources.label: Steam Guide
sources.what: Community 5-grade / G4 raw-material gathering guide for the shard run.

sources.label: EDISON / EDbearing
sources.what: Surface lat/long steering aids for reaching each stand-here coordinate.

sources.label: ed-nav
sources.what: Surface-navigation tool for plotting the shard-site lat/long circuit.

sources.label: Video guide
sources.what: Walkthrough of the shard-field DSS map, landing and SRV harvest.

sources.label: EDUtilities — Crystalline Shards
sources.what: Reference table & field imagery for crystalline-shard sites (shard-field photo in Section 4).

sources.label: EDUtilities — Brain Trees
sources.what: Reference table & imagery for brain-tree sites and seed-pod harvesting (brain-tree photo in Section 4).

sources.label: YouTube — Down to Earth Astronomy
sources.what: Walks the HIP 36601 shard fields, mapping which body drops which high-grade raw and the fastest collection loop.

sources.label: YouTube — Mile 13 Gaming
sources.what: Tutorial on gathering high-grade raw materials fast, covering crystal shards alongside the Jameson crash site and high-grade emissions.
